about.intro1: A mobile-friendly ZX Spectrum emulator for the browser. Source available <srcLink>here</srcLink>.
about.intro2: An alternative website, related to this one, is available at <coderLink>code.zxplay.org</coderLink>. ZX Coder supports running programs entered via web pages.
about.acknowledgementsIntro: This software uses code from the following open source projects:
about.sinclairRomText: Amstrad have kindly given their permission for the redistribution of their copyrighted material but retain that copyright.

linking.intro: You can link directly to ZX Play using URL parameters to load a program or game tape image from another URL.
linking.decompose: The URL can be decomposed in these parts:
linking.filtering: Optional filtering (default 0 is not filtered, good old pixels):
linking.buildOwn: You can build your own URL setting soft keys, machine type (defaults to 48) and a URL for a Z80, SNA, SZX, TZX or TAP file containing the desired game or program. Please note that the program/game URL to load must be hosted in a website with CORS enabled.
linking.syntaxSimple: The syntax is simple: keys are arranged as rows, and rows are separated by commas.
linking.threeRows: So, the previous strings has 3 rows:
linking.keyDefinition: A key is defined by its UPPERCASE character, and a hyphen (-) means a blank.
